articles.title: Hundreds arrested and police injured after Champions League riots in France
articles.summary: Nearly 800 people were arrested after clashes with police which saw 219 people injured, including 57 police officers.

articles.title: Brighton's Vidosic hopes to honour late father in FA Cup final
articles.summary: Brighton manager Dario Vidosic is hoping to honour his late father in Sunday's Women's FA Cup final when they take on Manchester City.

articles.title: Carra: An elite manager would have fixed Liverpool
articles.summary: Jamie Carragher was shocked by Arne Slot's sacking but believes an elite manager would have fixed Liverpool last season.

articles.title: Hundreds arrested in France as clashes disrupt PSG celebrations
articles.summary: Hundreds of arrests have been made across France in the aftermath of Paris Saint-Germain's Champions League final victory over Arsenal.

articles.title: Serrano stops Hanson in second round, Han beats Holm by decision in rematch
articles.summary: Amanda Serrano retained her unified featherweight world title and tied the all-time knockout record with a barnstorming second-round stoppage victory over Cheyenne Hanson in the MVP Women's co-main ev...

articles.title: 'He wanted to take it' - Gabriel's first Arsenal penalty ends with heartbreak
articles.summary: Gabriel's first penalty for Arsenal ends in heartache as his shootout miss hands Paris St-Germain a second successive Champions League title.

articles.title: PSG go back-to-back and join 'greatest of all time'
articles.summary: Paris St-Germain underline their status as one of European football's greatest ever teams by retaining their Champions League crown with victory against Arsenal.

articles.title: Arsenal 'pain' will fuel fire after Champions League heartbreak
articles.summary: On Sunday, Arsenal will get on an open bus parade to celebrate their Premier League title less than 24 hours after their Champions League final heartbreak.

articles.title: PSG retain Champions League with penalty shootout win over Arsenal
articles.summary: Arsenal lose a penalty shootout as Paris St-Germain win the Champions League for a second successive year.

articles.title: Slot sacking completes a remarkable fall from grace
articles.summary: 'Slot's is one of the most remarkable falls from grace in Premier League history' says chief football writer Phil McNulty.

articles.title: Gabriel blazes penalty over as PSG win Champions League
articles.summary: Paris St-Germain retain their Champions League crown as Arsenal defender Gabriel misses the decisive spot-kick in a penalty shootout.

articles.title: A team in need of change - why Liverpool parted with Slot
articles.summary: Why did Liverpool call time on head coach Arne Slot after two seasons in charge in which the club won the Premier League title?

articles.title: Decision not to award Arsenal penalty correct - analysis
articles.summary: Champions League analysts Nedum Onuoha and Pat Nevin both feel the decision to not award Arsenal a penalty in extra-time of the Champions League final against PSG was the correct one.

articles.title: Dembele equalises from penalty spot for PSG
articles.summary: PSG's Ousmane Dembele scores from the penalty spot to level the Champions League final against Arsenal in Budapest.

articles.title: Havertz fires Arsenal into early lead against PSG
articles.summary: Kai Havertz fires Arsenal into an early lead in the Champions League final against Paris St-Germain in Budapest.

articles.title: The Sunderland footballer setting record straight after 46 years
articles.summary: When Roly Gregoire quit football in 1980 after suffering a knee injury, he had faced so much racism that he could not bear to watch football for many years.
